messages.content: Reconfigure this job as a self-consistent photon–photon collider simulation at the stated center-of-mass energy, producing 5,000 events with seed 22. Ensure that the beam setup, photon treatment, and process selection are mutually compatible and do not rely on hadron–hadron soft-QCD machinery unless physically justified.

The allowed process list does not include any  (gamma–gamma) hard process, so a fully self-consistent photon–photon collider setup cannot be made runnable with the current constraints. I have reconfigured the beams and disabled hadron–hadron soft-QCD machinery, but without a supported  process the run is not recommended. If a gamma-gamma process can be added, I can finalize a runnable spec.
messages.proposal_summary: Switch beams to photons at 13 TeV, reduce events to 5,000 with seed 22, and disable hadron-hadron soft-QCD machinery and MPI/hadronization to align with a photon–photon collider concept. No compatible gamma–gamma process is available in the allowed list.
